title: Overtime vs. Hiring Breakeven Calculator
description: Industrial engineering tool to determine the breakeven point between using overtime and hiring additional full-time employees, incorporating labor costs, productivity, quality, and risk factors.

inputs.label: Current Headcount (FTEs)
inputs.businessContext: Number of full-time equivalent employees currently working in the department or line.

inputs.label: Average Hourly Wage (Base)
inputs.businessContext: Average base hourly wage for the role, excluding overtime premium.

inputs.label: Overtime Premium Multiplier
inputs.businessContext: Standard overtime pay multiplier (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double time).

inputs.label: Overtime Hours per Week (per FTE)
inputs.businessContext: Average overtime hours worked per full-time employee per week.

inputs.label: Hiring Cost per FTE
inputs.businessContext: Total cost to recruit, interview, onboard, and train a new employee (including agency fees, training materials, lost productivity).

inputs.label: Annual Benefits Cost per FTE
inputs.businessContext: Annual cost of benefits (health insurance, retirement, paid leave, etc.) per full-time employee.

inputs.label: Overtime Productivity Factor
inputs.businessContext: Productivity during overtime as a percentage of normal shift productivity (e.g., 85% due to fatigue).

inputs.label: New Hire Ramp-Up Productivity
inputs.businessContext: Average productivity of a new hire during the first 3 months as a percentage of an experienced employee.

inputs.label: Overtime Defect Rate Increase
inputs.businessContext: Additional defect rate (in percentage points) observed during overtime shifts due to fatigue.

inputs.label: Normal Defect Rate
inputs.businessContext: Baseline defect rate during regular shifts.

inputs.label: Cost per Defect (rework/scrap)
inputs.businessContext: Average cost to rework or scrap a defective unit (labor, materials, disposal).

inputs.label: Weekly Productive Hours Needed
inputs.businessContext: Total productive hours required per week to meet demand (adjusted for normal productivity).

inputs.label: Weeks per Year (operating)
inputs.businessContext: Number of weeks per year the operation runs (typically 52 for continuous, 50 for seasonal).

inputs.label: Include Risk Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, applies a risk multiplier to overtime costs to account for burnout, turnover, and safety incidents.

inputs.label: Overtime Risk Multiplier
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier applied to overtime costs when risk adjustment is enabled (based on historical incident data).

rules.message: Overtime hours must be greater than zero to calculate overtime scenario.
Current headcount must be at least 1.
Weekly productive hours needed must be greater than zero.
Risk multiplier must be >= 1.0 when risk adjustment is enabled.

thresholds.message: Overtime hours exceed 20/week per FTE – high risk of burnout and quality issues.
Breakeven hours negative – hiring is always cheaper than overtime under current parameters.
Defect costs in overtime scenario are more than 50% higher than hiring scenario – quality risk is significant.

formulas.label: Effective Regular Hours per FTE per Week
formulas.description: Productive hours contributed by one FTE during regular time (assuming 40-hour week at normal productivity).

formulas.label: Effective Overtime Hours per FTE per Week
formulas.description: Productive hours from overtime per FTE, adjusted for overtime productivity and defect rate.

formulas.label: Total Effective Hours per Week (Overtime Scenario)
formulas.description: Total productive hours available using current headcount plus overtime.

formulas.label: FTE Shortfall (if hiring)
formulas.description: Number of additional FTEs needed to meet weekly productive hours without overtime.

formulas.label: Annual Cost of Overtime Strategy
formulas.description: Total annual labor cost including base wages, overtime pay, and defect costs, with optional risk multiplier.
formulas.expression: current_headcount * (40 * avg_hourly_wage * weeks_per_year) + current_headcount * (overtime_hours_per_week * avg_hourly_wage * overtime_premium * weeks_per_year) + (current_headcount * overtime_hours_per_week * (quality_defect_rate_normal + quality_defect_rate_overtime) / 100 * cost_per_defect * weeks_per_year) * (include_risk_adjustment ? risk_multiplier : 1)

formulas.label: Annual Cost of Hiring Strategy
formulas.description: Total annual cost including base wages, benefits, hiring costs (amortized over first year), and defect costs for new hires.
formulas.expression: (current_headcount + fte_shortfall) * (40 * avg_hourly_wage * weeks_per_year + annual_benefits_cost_per_fte) + fte_shortfall * hiring_cost_per_fte + (current_headcount + fte_shortfall) * 40 * (quality_defect_rate_normal / 100) * cost_per_defect * weeks_per_year

formulas.label: Breakeven Overtime Hours per FTE per Week
formulas.description: The overtime hours per FTE at which the annual cost of overtime equals the annual cost of hiring. Derived by solving annual_cost_overtime = annual_cost_hiring for overtime_hours_per_week.
formulas.expression: (annual_cost_hiring - current_headcount * 40 * avg_hourly_wage * weeks_per_year) / (current_headcount * (avg_hourly_wage * overtime_premium * weeks_per_year + (quality_defect_rate_normal + quality_defect_rate_overtime) / 100 * cost_per_defect * weeks_per_year * (include_risk_adjustment ? risk_multiplier : 1)))

primary.label: Breakeven Overtime Hours per FTE per Week
primary.description: If actual overtime hours exceed this value, hiring is more cost-effective. If below, overtime is cheaper.

cost_difference.label: Cost Difference (Hiring – Overtime)
cost_difference.description: Positive means overtime is cheaper; negative means hiring is cheaper.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Fatigue-Induced Quality Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional defect costs due to overtime fatigue, calculated as overtime_hours_per_week * current_headcount * quality_defect_rate_overtime / 100 * cost_per_defect * weeks_per_year.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: New Hire Ramp-Up Productivity Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Productivity gap during new hire ramp-up period (first 3 months), estimated as fte_shortfall * 40 * (1 - productivity_factor_new_hire / 100) * 12 weeks * avg_hourly_wage.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Overtime Burnout & Turnover Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated cost of increased turnover due to chronic overtime, using a standard 15% turnover rate increase per 10 hours overtime/week.

suggestedActions.action: If breakeven_hours < actual overtime hours, initiate hiring process for fte_shortfall FTEs.
Implement fatigue management protocols (job rotation, micro-breaks) to reduce overtime defect rate.
Review overtime risk multiplier annually using incident and turnover data.
Consider cross-training to increase effective regular hours per FTE and reduce overtime dependency.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Confidence-Adjusted Breakeven
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Breakeven hours adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input variability. Default confidence factor = 1.0.
